field.label: Documentation
field.help:
Kea Unbound DDNS Integration

Bridges Kea DHCP and Unbound DNS so hostnames resolve the moment a lease is issued, without waiting for a DNS reload or manual entry.

How It Works

DDNS Listener (real-time): Receives RFC 2136 DNS UPDATE packets from kea-dhcp-ddns on 127.0.0.1:{{ port }} and immediately registers or removes A/AAAA and PTR records in Unbound. Records managed by Unbound Host Overrides are never touched.

Self-healing consistency: The resident daemon watches Kea and Unbound service pidfiles. Whenever either service restarts (flushing runtime DNS records), the daemon automatically runs a full reconcile from Kea — repopulating Unbound without any manual intervention or PHP configure hook. The status banner above shows the current reconcile state.

Static Reservation Sync: Registers Kea static reservations in Unbound before any client connects. Runs as part of each full reconcile and on demand via the manual Sync button on the Lease Audit tab.

Active Lease Sync: Bulk-syncs all active Kea leases into Unbound with TTLs matching remaining lease time. Runs as part of each full reconcile and on demand via the manual Sync button on the Lease Audit tab.

DNS Record Cleaning: Periodically removes stale records not backed by any active Kea lease, reservation, or Unbound Host Override. See the DNS Record Cleaning settings below. The Lease Audit tab shows a full preview of what each cleanup would remove.

Configuring Kea for DDNS

Kea's DHCP-DDNS daemon must be running and forwarding updates to 127.0.0.1:{{ port }}. For each subnet, add to the Kea subnet config:

"ddns-send-updates": true,
"ddns-override-no-update": true,
"ddns-override-client-update": true,
"hostname-char-replacement": "-"

Use the Config Check tab to verify Unbound and Kea DHCP configuration.

Tabs

  • Settings: Enable the plugin; configure sync, cleanup, and listen port
  • Config Check: Verify Unbound DNS settings and Kea subnet DDNS configuration
  • Lease Audit: Inspect all DNS records, preview and run cleanup, trigger syncs
  • Log File: Daemon, sync, audit, and cleanup logs

field.label: Enabled
field.help: Enable Kea to Unbound DNS registration. When enabled, DNS UPDATE packets from kea-dhcp-ddns are received and translated into Unbound local_data entries.

field.label: Synthesize PTR records
field.help: Automatically create the standard in-addr.arpa / ip6.arpa PTR (reverse) record for every A/AAAA update received from kea-dhcp-ddns. Enabled by default — PTRs work immediately without needing a reverse zone in kea-dhcp-ddns. Disable only if you manage reverse DNS separately (e.g. another authoritative server for in-addr.arpa). Explicit PTR updates from kea-dhcp-ddns are always applied regardless of this setting. When both this option and a kea-dhcp-ddns reverse zone are configured for a subnet, PTRs are written by both paths — harmless, but the Config Check tab will flag it per subnet.

field.label: Hostname collision policy
field.help:
Action when a hostname is already registered in Unbound for a different IP address.

Last wins (default): the existing record is removed and replaced by the new one. Correct behaviour for most networks — Kea issues the new IP and DDNS updates Unbound atomically.
Allow: both records coexist — Unbound will round-robin between them. Use when you want multiple IPs per hostname (e.g. dual-stack hosts with separate DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 leases).
First wins: the existing record is kept; the new registrant is rejected. Static reservations are always synced before dynamic leases, so First wins naturally protects reservation DNS entries from being overwritten by dynamic clients. Best-effort on reconcile after restart (ordering is deterministic within a run, not across restarts).
None: on any dynamic conflict, no dynamic lease gets the bare hostname — existing dynamic records for that name are deleted. The hostname becomes available again once all competing leases expire and a new lease registers. Useful when hostname conflicts indicate misconfiguration and you want conflicts to be silent rather than resolved arbitrarily.

Note: this plugin does not store or validate DHCID records. Collision detection is based on name-to-IP conflicts in Unbound, not client identity.

field.label: Magic hostnames
field.help:
When enabled, every host involved in a hostname collision receives an additional disambiguated forward record alongside whatever the collision policy determines for the bare hostname. The magic hostname encodes the hardware identifier type and last 6 characters of the identifier:

laptop-mAABBCC — MAC-identified host (last 3 octets)
laptop-dAABBCC — DUID-identified host
laptop-i001050 — IP-only host override (last 2 octets, 3-padded)

Magic hostnames are not removed eagerly when a collision resolves — they persist until the lease expires naturally, so cached references (SSH sessions, monitoring) remain valid. Enable LAA tagging below to mark devices using randomized MACs (iOS, Android) with an extra -laa- infix.

field.label: Magic PTR records
field.help:
When enabled, PTR (reverse) records for dynamic lease IPs in a collision group point to the magic FQDN rather than the bare hostname. Every IP in the group gets a stable, unambiguous PTR that never changes as collision winners rotate:

101.1.168.192.in-addr.arpa PTR laptop-mAABBCC.home.lan.

When disabled (default), PTR behaviour is unchanged from non-magic mode: the collision winner gets PTR to the bare hostname, losers get no PTR record. The mixed state (winner bare, losers magic) is deliberately avoided — PTR targets would otherwise change each time the collision winner rotates.

Requires both Magic hostnames and Synthesize PTR records to be enabled. Note: magic PTRs cause a mismatch between the hostname in a TLS certificate (issued for the bare hostname) and the PTR returned by a reverse lookup, which can break tools that perform reverse-lookup certificate verification.

field.label: LAA tagging for magic hostnames
field.help:
When enabled alongside magic hostnames, devices whose MAC address has the locally administered bit set (IEEE 802c / RFC 9724) receive an -laa- infix in their magic hostname:

laptop-laa-mAABBCC

LAA MACs are assigned by the device itself (not the manufacturer) and are commonly used for MAC randomization on iOS, Android, and Windows. The infix signals that the suffix may not be stable across reconnections — the device may rotate its MAC per network or per day. Do not use LAA magic hostnames in long-lived references such as TLS certificates or SSH known_hosts entries.

field.label: Clean stale records after restart
field.help: After each full reconcile triggered by a Kea or Unbound restart, sweep Unbound for records no longer backed by any Kea reservation or active lease and remove them immediately. Useful when static reservations are removed — the stale DNS records disappear on the next restart rather than waiting for the scheduled auto-clean.

field.label: Enable scheduled sync / clean
field.help: Run Kea→Unbound sync and/or stale-record cleaning on a schedule. Use the sub-options below to choose what runs. The frequency and hour fields below control when jobs fire.

field.label: Full sync
field.help: Reconcile all Kea leases and reservations into Unbound at each scheduled interval. Catches any drift the live path or log watcher missed — including new leases registered while kea-dhcp-ddns was temporarily down. Only active when scheduled sync / clean is enabled.

field.label: Clean stale records
field.help: Remove Unbound records not backed by any active Kea lease, reservation, or Unbound Host Override — primarily records left behind by leases that expired without an explicit release. Review the Lease Audit tab to preview what would be removed. Only active when scheduled sync / clean is enabled.

field.label: Schedule frequency
field.help: How often to run the scheduled sync and/or clean. The manual Sync and Clean buttons on the Lease Audit tab run the same operations on demand.

field.label: Schedule hour (0–23)
field.help: Hour of the day (0–23, UTC) for the daily scheduled run. Only used when frequency is set to "Daily".

field.label: Enable log watcher
field.help: Enable a secondary daemon that tails the Kea DHCP log and the listener log for events the live DDNS path missed. Use the sub-options below to choose which events trigger action. Requires Kea's kea-dhcp4 / kea-dhcp6 loggers at INFO severity or lower writing to /var/log/kea/ — the OPNsense default. Off by default — the scheduled sync covers most deployments without it.

field.label: Purge IP on lease release
field.help: When a DHCP4_RELEASE or DHCP6_RELEASE is seen in the Kea log, immediately purge all DNS records for that IP. This is the fastest path to removing stale forward and reverse records when a device explicitly releases its lease. Only active when Log Watcher is enabled.

field.label: Re-sync names after listener SERVFAIL
field.help: When the DDNS listener returns SERVFAIL (Unbound briefly unavailable or locked during an update), trigger a targeted kea-sync for the affected names. Supplements the dirty-name drain so missed adds and removes recover quickly rather than waiting for the next scheduled sync. Only active when Log Watcher is enabled.

field.label: Re-sync on missed DNS removes
field.help: When a DNS Remove is seen in the listener log without a corresponding Add within a short grace window, trigger a targeted kea-sync for that hostname. Closes the LEASE_REUSE gap: when kea-dhcp-ddns sends a CHG_REMOVE for a hostname/IP and then skips the follow-up Add (because the lease looks unchanged to D2), the DNS record disappears until the next scheduled sync. With this option on, the gap is closed within seconds. Only active when Log Watcher is enabled.

field.label: Port
field.help: UDP port to listen on for DNS UPDATE packets from kea-dhcp-ddns. Must match the destination port configured in kea-dhcp-ddns. Default 53535.

field.label: Max reconcile attempts
field.help: How many failed full reconcile attempts are allowed before the daemon marks itself degraded (alert banner) and stops retrying. 0 means retry indefinitely. Default 5.

field.label: Readiness watchdog (minutes)
field.help: Minutes to wait for Kea and Unbound to become ready after startup before the watchdog terminates the daemon (daemon(8) will restart it). 0 disables the watchdog. Default 10.

field.label: Enable daemon-triggered recovery
field.help: After the NCR threshold below is reached, the daemon calls unbound-control fast-reload to reclaim heap memory fragmented by repeated local_data add and remove calls, then immediately runs a full Kea sync to repopulate records. Falls back to unbound-control reload on Unbound older than 1.22. This controls only the daemon NCR counter — the scheduled recovery job below is independent and has its own toggle.

field.label: NCR threshold
field.help: Number of live-path DNS UPDATE packets (NCRs) returning NOERROR before the daemon triggers a fast-reload. Each NCR typically generates 1-3 unbound-control calls. Range 100-100000. Default 5000. Only active when fast-reload is enabled.

field.label: Enable scheduled recovery
field.help: Run a cron job that triggers fast-reload on a fixed schedule, independent of the daemon NCR counter. Catches the case where the daemon restarts (resetting its counter) but Unbound keeps running, allowing memory to accumulate indefinitely.

field.label: Scheduled recovery interval
field.help: How often the scheduled recovery cron job fires. Only active when scheduled recovery is enabled.

field.label: Scheduled recovery time (hour)
field.help: Hour of day (0–23) at which the scheduled recovery cron job fires. Default is 3 (03:00). Only active when scheduled recovery is enabled.
